title: Signs and Symptoms of Concussion
license_notes: Use as public government source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Concussion symptoms may not appear right away and can change during recovery.
Emergency danger signs include seizure, repeated vomiting, worsening headache, confusion, trouble waking, slurred speech, weakness, numbness, coordination problems, or vision changes.
Concussion signs and symptoms vary by age and clinical context.
